advantages: Enables binary search and many range/window algorithms downstream.
Python's Timsort is stable and adapts to already-sorted runs — often beating textbook O(n log n) on real data.
Stability lets you build multi-key orderings cheaply by sorting multiple times.
Out-of-core sorting (mergesort) handles datasets larger than memory.
disadvantages: Full sorting is O(n log n); for top-k or running-rank workloads a heap is cheaper.
Quicksort's worst case is O(n²) on adversarial inputs.
Some sorts (mergesort) need O(n) extra memory.
Stability is not free — non-stable sorts can be faster but reorder equal-keyed rows.
